mapping.a_element: weak target signal submerged in background noise
mapping.b_element: sentiment signal to be recovered from filing text

mapping.a_element: signal-to-noise ratio of the detection protocol
mapping.b_element: amount of independent training signal available at each aggregation level

brief_justification: The only overlap is the generic framing of extracting a target signal from noise; the actual machinery differs entirely. Paper A uses quantum adiabatic evolution to implement triangular-wave modulation and lock-in demodulation (a physical spectral-filtering technique that suppresses spectral leakage), whereas Paper B uses supervised lexicon-learning that regresses word scores against return/volatility labels across aggregation levels. There is no role-for-role correspondence between adiabatic modulation/demodulation and statistical sentiment regression, so no shared underlying method holds.
